title: mentha longifolia subsp. modesta
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. modesta, also known as Long-Leaved Peppermint or Hornmint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, a perennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It is characterized by its long, narrow leaves and purple flowers, often used in herbal medicine due to its menthol and menthone content, which possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Mentha longifolia subsp. modesta are believed to provide relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall health and wellness. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal benefits.
